+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-9CF3-S0X8-R38F-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:39ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across centralNebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: BUILDING A SUSTAINABLE FUTURE TOGETHER

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHD-M503-S6WK-11F0-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:39ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across central Nebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JH6-N063-SC5B-N0DW-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT PEER NEWS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:39ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across centralNebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-4M53-RXNT-64JM-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:39ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across central Nebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHD-SVN3-S56J-J2FC-00000-00WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:39ZLexisNexisSTOCKWALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY - SECTOR INSIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY

PermID5000066406Websitehttp://www.walbridge.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress777 Woodward Ave., Ste. 300 Detroit, MI 48226

ACTIVITIES:

Walbridge Aldinger Company, founded in 1916, is a construction management firm headquartered in Detroit, Michigan. With over 1,500 employees, it serves automotive, commercial, and industrial sectors, delivering projects like manufacturing plants and data centers. Generating over $2 billion annually, Walbridge is known for its lean construction practices and safety focus. In 2024, it expanded its data center and renewable energy project portfolio, capitalizing on tech infrastructure demand. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and employee development, with strong community engagement initiatives. Competing with Gilbane, Walbridge stands out for its automotive expertise and client-centric approach. Recent initiatives include investments in sustainable construction and digital project management tools, reinforcing its industry leadership.

+ WALBRIDGE ALDINGER COMPANY [1 Press Release]

+ WALBRIDGE IMPROVES ECOVADIS SCORE, RANKING AMONG TOP-RATED COMPANIES IN 2025

Building on the foundation Walbridge has established, our sustainability journey continues to gain momentum. Our 2025 EcoVadis assessment score increased from 60 to 67, placing us in the 77th percentile among more than 150,000 companies rated worldwide. This year's results reflect meaningful progress across several key sustainability categories and reinforce our standing as one of the top-rated companies in our industry and among companies of similar size.

EcoVadis is a globally recognized platform for measuring sustainability performance across business operations. The assessment reviews 21 criteria across Environment, Labor and Human Rights, Ethics and Sustainable Procurement. We continue to leverage EcoVadis feedback to refine our sustainability strategies and strengthen areas for continued progress.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [4 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW + SHERIDAN CONSTRUCTION SET TO BUILD NEW MACON ARENA

MACON, GEORGIA - Barton Malow, in association with Sheridan Construction, has been chosen as the Construction Manager for the new Macon Arena, a major project that will replace the current Macon Coliseum and serve as a key part of the region's expanding entertainment opportunities.

Located southeast of Atlanta in Macon-Bibb County, the planned facility is designed to host various multipurpose events like minor league hockey, basketball games, high school tournaments, concerts, conferences, trade shows, and more. The new arena will offer a top-tier guest experience, featuring premium seating options like luxury suites, club suites, and loge boxes, as well as modern fan amenities such as clubs, concessions, restrooms, locker rooms, and integrated technologies. Back-of-house and support spaces will ensure the venue functions as a state-of-the-art civic facility, offering performers, event planners, and organizations a great experience.

"This arena won't be just a venue, it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come," said Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.

As Macon's entertainment landscape continues to expand with the new Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium, the new arena is poised to further elevate the region's growing lineup of elite venues.

"We're thrilled to partner with Sheridan Construction and bring Barton Malow's expertise to Macon-Bibb to deliver a top-tier arena. The new venue is set to become a central location in Georgia for sports, entertainment, and community gatherings," said Barton Malow Office Director Blake Boyd. "This project is an investment in Macon-Bibb County's future, and we're excited to support the local community every step of the way."

Adds Sheridan Construction President + CEO Christy Kovac. "It's a privilege for our firm to return to Macon's legacy of entertainment and civic pride, having built the original coliseum 57 years ago. Partnering with Barton Malow on this transformative new arena is both an honor and a responsibility. We believe this project will serve as a powerful economic and tourism engine, attracting visitors, creating opportunities, and enriching the lives of everyone in our community for generations to come."

Construction is planned to commence in summer 2026, with the project anticipated to reach completion in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY LUKE KNOCHEL PROMOTED TO VICE PRESIDENT OF PROJECT CONTROLS

Luke's project controls leadership role spans multiple geographic regions and consists of overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Barton Malow has announced the promotion of Luke Knochel to Vice President of Project Controls.

In his new role, Luke leads a multi‑disciplinary team responsible for core functions such as cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start‑up, accounts receivable and payable processes, and change management. His leadership spans multiple geographic regions, overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Luke started at Barton Malow in 2013 as a Cost Analyst and has served in various project controls and finance leadership roles throughout his tenure. He oversaw accounts payable and accounts receivable, change management, forecasting, reporting, and more on the Little Caesars Arena project, then went on to supervise Barton Malow's offices in Chicago, Columbus, and Nashville. After a promotion to Project Controls Manager overseeing the then-Midwest business unit, Luke transitioned into a corporate finance role managing the shared service department and implementing new accounts payable software.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY BUILDING FOR THE BRAND: CONSTRUCTING COLLEGE ATHLETIC FACILITIES IN THE NIL ERA

NIL athletic construction: Many modern facilities now include dedicated content creation zones where athletes can produce high-quality photos and videos for social media and sponsorship campaigns.

On July 1, 2021, the NCAA formally adopted a policy allowing college athletes to earn money for their name, image, and likeness (NIL).

At the time, one of my acquaintances, Matt Wilson, was serving as the Senior Director of Development for Athletics at Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University. He witnessed firsthand the immediate impact that NIL had on college athletics.

Today, Matt sees things from the other side of the table. After leaving Embry-Riddle in 2022, he spent two years as Executive Director of NIL Collective Operations + Development at Athlete Forward Initiative. Now he runs his own consulting group designed to provide leadership, fundraising, communication, and culture development training for college athletic departments.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW EARNS ECOVADIS BRONZE MEDAL FOR SUSTAINABILITY EXCELLENCE

Barton Malow has been awarded the EcoVadis Bronze Medal, placing the construction enterprise among the top 35% of companies worldwide for sustainability performance. This achievement underscores Barton Malow's leadership in green building practices, ethical business operations, and sustainable procurement.

"This recognition marks a significant milestone in our ongoing commitment to integrating sustainability principles across our business and reflects the progress we've made in partnership with our clients and stakeholders," said Senior Director of Sustainability Jill Katic.

EcoVadis, the world's most trusted provider of business sustainability ratings, evaluates companies on four key themes: Environment, Labor & Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement.

Barton Malow is committed to creating a positive impact on the planet, its people, and the communities in which it builds. To learn more about how Barton Malow is working towards its long-term environmental commitments, like reducing carbon emissions and implementing green building practices, explore the 2025 Sustainability Impact Report.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER SELECTED FOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT ORLANDO MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT

The 900,000-square-foot project developed by JMA Ventures will create a vibrant destination in downtown Orlando.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Throughout April, including Earth Day on April 22, Turner International Turkiye celebrated Earth Month across all 8 workplaces with a series of inspiring activities focused on sustainability and environmental awareness.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.
